symptoms: unsure which package to install
confused between @xenova/transformers and @huggingface/transformers
root_cause: Transformers.js v3+ moved to the @huggingface/transformers npm scope. The legacy @xenova/transformers package is v2 and lacks WebGPU and the dtype quantization API.
fix: For new projects install the v3 package: npm install @huggingface/transformers
Import as: import { pipeline } from '@huggingface/transformers'
Only fall back to @xenova/transformers if you need to ship code that already targets the v2 API surface

symptoms: want a minimal hello-world
first time using pipeline()
root_cause: The pipeline() helper wraps tokenizer + model + post-processing for a task and is the recommended entry point for most use cases.
fix: import { pipeline } from '@huggingface/transformers'
const classifier = await pipeline('sentiment-analysis')
const out = await classifier('I love Transformers.js!')
// out -> [{ label: 'POSITIVE', score: 0.999... }]

symptoms: want to choose a specific model instead of the task default
default model is too large or too small
root_cause: Each task has a default model on the Hub, but you usually want to pin an explicit one for reproducibility, smaller size, or quantization variant.
fix: Pass a model id as the second argument: await pipeline('automatic-speech-recognition', 'onnx-community/whisper-base')
Pin a revision in production with the { revision: 'main' } option for reproducible loads
Browse onnx-community/* and Xenova/* on huggingface.co for ONNX-ready models

symptoms: want to load models from a local /models folder
production build must work offline
root_cause: By default Transformers.js fetches models from the Hugging Face Hub. For offline or pinned deployments you set env.allowLocalModels and env.localModelPath.
fix: import { env } from '@huggingface/transformers'
env.allowLocalModels = true
env.allowRemoteModels = false
env.localModelPath = '/models/' // served from public/ in Next.js or static assets
Place ONNX files under public/models/<org>/<model>/onnx/ matching the Hub layout

symptoms: want to disable remote model downloads
block accidental Hub fetches
root_cause: env.allowRemoteModels controls whether the runtime is allowed to reach out to huggingface.co. Disable it when you ship all weights with your app.
fix: env.allowRemoteModels = false
Combine with env.allowLocalModels = true and env.localModelPath
Verify the network panel shows zero requests to huggingface.co after the first cold load

symptoms: want to choose between WebGPU and WASM
model is too slow on CPU
root_cause: Transformers.js v3 supports a `device` option that selects the ONNX Runtime Web execution provider. WebGPU is much faster than WASM but is not available on every browser/GPU.
fix: Try WebGPU first: await pipeline(task, model, { device: 'webgpu' })
Fall back to WASM if WebGPU init fails: catch the error and re-create with { device: 'wasm' }
Feature-detect: if (!('gpu' in navigator)) use 'wasm'

symptoms: want to pick a quantization level
model file is too large to download
root_cause: v3 exposes a `dtype` option that selects which quantized variant of the ONNX model to download (q4, q8, fp16, fp32). Lower precision means smaller download and faster CPU inference at some accuracy cost.
fix: await pipeline(task, model, { dtype: 'q8' }) // ~4x smaller than fp32, good default
For tiny CPUs try 'q4' (smallest) and measure quality regression
Use 'fp16' on WebGPU for the best speed/accuracy trade-off

symptoms: want different dtypes per encoder/decoder
encoder is fine in fp16 but decoder needs q4
root_cause: Encoder-decoder models (Whisper, T5, BART) accept a dtype map so you can quantize each submodule separately.
fix: await pipeline('automatic-speech-recognition', 'onnx-community/whisper-base', { dtype: { encoder_model: 'fp16', decoder_model_merged: 'q4' } })
Inspect <model>/onnx/ on the Hub to see which file names exist
Match map keys to filenames without the .onnx extension

symptoms: want a progress bar while a model downloads
users sit on a blank screen during first load
root_cause: pipeline() and AutoModel.from_pretrained() accept a progress_callback that fires for every file fetch and progress update.
fix: const pipe = await pipeline(task, model, { progress_callback: (p) => self.postMessage(p) })
p has shape { status: 'progress', file, progress, loaded, total }
Render a progress bar from p.progress (0..100)

symptoms: model is loaded multiple times
memory grows on every call
root_cause: Each call to pipeline() loads weights again. Cache the resolved instance and reuse it across requests.
fix: Use a singleton: let _pipe; export async function getPipe() { return _pipe ??= await pipeline(task, model) }
In a worker, hold the instance in module scope; never create per-message
Dispose the previous one with await pipe.dispose() before swapping models

symptoms: want text generation streaming token by token
long generation feels frozen
root_cause: Use TextStreamer to receive tokens as they are decoded; this is the canonical streaming UX for LLMs in the browser.
fix: import { pipeline, TextStreamer } from '@huggingface/transformers'
const generator = await pipeline('text-generation', model, { dtype: 'q4', device: 'webgpu' })
const streamer = new TextStreamer(generator.tokenizer, { skip_prompt: true, callback_function: (txt) => self.postMessage({ type: 'token', txt }) })
await generator(prompt, { max_new_tokens: 256, streamer })

symptoms: need image classification
want to label an uploaded photo
root_cause: The image-classification pipeline accepts URLs, Blobs, and HTMLImageElement directly via RawImage.
fix: const classifier = await pipeline('image-classification', 'Xenova/vit-base-patch16-224')
const out = await classifier(imageUrlOrBlob, { topk: 5 })
For File objects: const url = URL.createObjectURL(file); call classifier(url); URL.revokeObjectURL(url) afterwards

symptoms: want sentence embeddings for similarity search
need to compute cosine similarity in the browser
root_cause: feature-extraction returns last hidden states; pass { pooling: 'mean', normalize: true } to get usable sentence embeddings.
fix: const embed = await pipeline('feature-extraction', 'Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2')
const e = await embed('hello world', { pooling: 'mean', normalize: true })
Use e.data (Float32Array, 384 dims) directly with a dot product to compute cosine similarity

symptoms: want zero-shot text classification
no labelled data
root_cause: zero-shot-classification uses NLI models to score candidate labels for a sentence without fine-tuning.
fix: const z = await pipeline('zero-shot-classification', 'Xenova/distilbert-base-uncased-mnli')
const out = await z('I love programming!', ['technology', 'sports', 'cooking'])
Set { multi_label: true } when more than one label may apply

symptoms: want translation in the browser
need offline translation
root_cause: translation pipeline supports many language pairs via NLLB or marian models.
fix: const translator = await pipeline('translation', 'Xenova/nllb-200-distilled-600M')
await translator('Hello, world!', { src_lang: 'eng_Latn', tgt_lang: 'fra_Latn' })
NLLB language codes use the FLORES-200 ISO 639-3 + script convention

symptoms: first model load is very slow
cold start downloads gigabytes
root_cause: On the first run the runtime downloads the model and stores it in IndexedDB; subsequent loads are fast. Pick the smallest dtype that meets your accuracy bar to shrink the cold start.
fix: Prefer q4 or q8 dtype for first-load critical paths
Show a progress UI and warm the model behind a 'Try the demo' click so users opt into the download
Preload weights from a service worker on idle if they are committed assets

symptoms: want to verify a model is being served from cache
not sure if IndexedDB is working
root_cause: Transformers.js stores weights in IndexedDB under a database named 'transformers-cache' (and Cache Storage for some versions). Watch the Network panel and Application > IndexedDB tab in DevTools.
fix: DevTools > Network: cold load shows fetches to huggingface.co; second load shows 0 model requests
DevTools > Application > Storage > IndexedDB > transformers-cache to inspect cached files
Set env.useBrowserCache = true (default) to enable caching; set false to force every load to refetch

symptoms: want to clear the cached models programmatically
users report stale model after upgrade
root_cause: Models are keyed by URL. Bumping the model id or revision invalidates the cache. You can also delete the IndexedDB database directly.
fix: Pin a revision and bump it to roll users forward: { revision: '2024-06-01' }
To wipe everything: indexedDB.deleteDatabase('transformers-cache') and reload
Expose a 'Clear local model cache' button in the UI for support

symptoms: Error: no available backend found. ERR: [wasm] backend not found
ONNX Runtime Web fails to initialise
root_cause: The wasm files (ort-wasm*.wasm/jsep) for ONNX Runtime Web couldn't be located by the loader. This usually means the bundler isn't copying them or the wasmPaths URL is wrong.
fix: Set env.backends.onnx.wasm.wasmPaths to a URL that serves the .wasm files (e.g. '/onnx/' if you copied node_modules/onnxruntime-web/dist into public/onnx/)
For a CDN: env.backends.onnx.wasm.wasmPaths = 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/onnxruntime-web@<version>/dist/'
Verify in DevTools that ort-wasm-simd-threaded.jsep.wasm is reachable (HTTP 200, content-type application/wasm)

symptoms: WASM threads not enabled
warnings about SharedArrayBuffer
root_cause: ONNX Runtime Web threaded WASM needs SharedArrayBuffer, which requires cross-origin isolation (COOP + COEP headers).
fix: Send Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin and Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy: require-corp on every document response
Verify with self.crossOriginIsolated === true in the browser console
If you cannot enable COOP/COEP, set env.backends.onnx.wasm.numThreads = 1 to silence the warnings and accept slower inference

symptoms: WASM SIMD not used
inference slower than expected
root_cause: Older browsers without WebAssembly SIMD fall back to scalar WASM, which is markedly slower. The runtime auto-selects but you can pin and inspect.
fix: Pin SIMD on: env.backends.onnx.wasm.simd = true
Confirm in DevTools that ort-wasm-simd*.wasm (not ort-wasm.wasm) is loaded

symptoms: want to control how many WASM threads run
tab pegs CPU at 100%
root_cause: env.backends.onnx.wasm.numThreads controls the worker pool size for ONNX Runtime Web. Too many threads on a low-core machine causes contention.
fix: Default to navigator.hardwareConcurrency / 2 to leave room for the UI
env.backends.onnx.wasm.numThreads = Math.min(4, Math.max(1, Math.floor((navigator.hardwareConcurrency || 4) / 2)))
Set to 1 if cross-origin isolation isn't available

symptoms: TypeError: WebAssembly.instantiate(): Out of memory
RangeError: WebAssembly.Memory(): could not allocate memory
root_cause: Running a model larger than the WASM 32-bit address space allows (4 GB hard cap, ~2 GB practical). On mobile the limit is much lower and OOM kills the page.
fix: Switch to a smaller dtype (q8 or q4) to halve/quarter memory
Pick a smaller model (whisper-tiny instead of whisper-large)
Move to WebGPU which uses GPU memory and is not bound by the WASM heap
Release previous instances with await pipe.dispose() before loading a new model

symptoms: want to use a custom CDN for model files
Hub is blocked by corporate firewall
root_cause: env.remoteHost and env.remotePathTemplate let you redirect model fetches to your own mirror.
fix: env.remoteHost = 'https://cdn.example.com'
env.remotePathTemplate = '{model}/resolve/{revision}/'
Mirror the same path layout as huggingface.co (e.g. /<org>/<model>/resolve/main/onnx/model.onnx)

symptoms: Error: Could not locate file: "https://huggingface.co/.../onnx/model.onnx"
404 on model file
root_cause: The model id you passed has no ONNX export at the expected path (most Hub models are PyTorch only).
fix: Pick a model from onnx-community/* or Xenova/* — those organisations publish ONNX exports
Search the Hub with library:transformers.js to filter to compatible models
If you must use a PyTorch-only model, convert it with the optimum CLI: optimum-cli export onnx --model <id> ./out

symptoms: want to convert a PyTorch model to ONNX for Transformers.js
no ONNX export available on the Hub
root_cause: Hugging Face Optimum exports PyTorch / TensorFlow models to ONNX in the layout Transformers.js expects.
fix: pip install 'optimum[exporters]'
optimum-cli export onnx --model <hub-or-local> --task <task> ./out
Optionally quantize: optimum-cli onnxruntime quantize --avx2 -o ./out-q8 ./out
Upload ./out to your own Hub repo or serve from your CDN

symptoms: want to keep inference off the main thread
UI freezes during model run
root_cause: The browser is single-threaded for JS on the main thread; running a model there blocks paint and input. The canonical fix is a Web Worker.
fix: Create a worker file: const worker = new Worker(new URL('./worker.ts', import.meta.url), { type: 'module' })
Inside the worker import Transformers.js and hold the pipeline as a singleton
Use postMessage on both ends; never share DOM nodes between threads

symptoms: want a typed message protocol between worker and UI
string switch in onmessage is fragile
root_cause: Using a tagged-union message type (TypeScript discriminated unions) keeps the protocol typed across the postMessage boundary.
fix: type Req = { type: 'load' } | { type: 'transcribe', audio: Float32Array, lang: string }
type Res = { type: 'progress', file: string, progress: number } | { type: 'done', text: string } | { type: 'error', message: string }
Cast in onmessage: const msg = e.data as Req; switch (msg.type) { ... }

symptoms: Float32Array copies between main and worker are slow
UI lags posting long audio buffers
root_cause: postMessage structured-clones by default. For large typed arrays, transfer them so ownership moves with no copy.
fix: worker.postMessage({ type: 'audio', buffer: f32 }, [f32.buffer])
After transfer, f32 on the sender is detached (length 0) — only access it from the receiver
Use SharedArrayBuffer when both sides need read access (requires COOP/COEP)

symptoms: ReferenceError: window is not defined inside a worker
code uses document.* in a module that runs in a worker
root_cause: Web Workers have no DOM. Any code path that touches window or document throws when imported in the worker.
fix: Move DOM-touching code out of the worker module — keep the worker side strictly inference + audio buffers
Replace window references with self (the worker global) when an alias is needed
Lazy-import shared utilities that branch on typeof window

symptoms: Vite build fails with 'fs', 'path', 'crypto' externals
browser bundle tries to import Node built-ins
root_cause: Some onnxruntime-web / sharp dependency paths reference Node-only modules. Vite normally polyfills nothing.
fix: In vite.config.ts add resolve.alias to map Node modules to empty: resolve: { alias: { fs: false, path: false, crypto: false } } via vite-plugin-node-polyfills set to disable
Or configure optimizeDeps.exclude: ['onnxruntime-web']
Confirm with import.meta.env.SSR === false that the module is browser-only

symptoms: Webpack: Module not found: Can't resolve 'fs' / 'path' / 'crypto'
Next.js client build fails on first import of @huggingface/transformers
root_cause: ONNX Runtime Web's optional Node code paths still appear in the dependency graph; Webpack 5 doesn't auto-polyfill Node built-ins.
fix: Set webpack.resolve.fallback for the client build only: { fs: false, path: false, crypto: false }
In Next.js: webpack(config, { isServer }) { if (!isServer) { config.resolve.fallback = { ...config.resolve.fallback, fs: false, path: false, crypto: false } } return config }
Do NOT set the fallback for the server bundle; leave Node built-ins available there

symptoms: @huggingface/transformers throws on the server
Next.js page errors during SSR with the worker import
root_cause: Transformers.js code paths reach for browser-only globals (Worker, navigator) and crash during SSR.
fix: Wrap the consuming component with next/dynamic and disable SSR: const Audio = dynamic(() => import('@/components/AudioInterface'), { ssr: false })
Never call pipeline() at module top level; only inside a useEffect or worker
Mark the client component with "use client" if you need React-side state around it

symptoms: Vite 'new Worker(new URL(...), { type: "module" })' breaks in production build
worker file is missing after build
root_cause: Vite needs the new URL(..., import.meta.url) pattern to detect and emit the worker chunk; any deviation skips emission.
fix: Always write: new Worker(new URL('./worker.ts', import.meta.url), { type: 'module' })
Don't compute the URL with string concat or template literals
For Webpack 5 the same pattern works; for Next.js it works under app/ when 'use client' is set

symptoms: warning: Failed to fetch dynamically imported module
code splitting broken for the worker
root_cause: After a deploy, an older cached HTML can request a worker chunk hash that no longer exists. Service workers and aggressive caching make this worse.
fix: Use immutable cache headers on hashed assets and short cache on HTML
Catch dynamic import failures and prompt the user to reload
Pin the worker URL to a stable public/ path if you need long-lived clients

symptoms: want to disable progress logs in production
console is noisy with file load lines
root_cause: Transformers.js logs progress callbacks by default. Set env.debug or replace the logger.
fix: env.backends.onnx.logSeverityLevel = 3 // 0=verbose, 4=fatal
Provide your own progress_callback so the default console logger is bypassed
Wrap pipeline() in a try/catch and log only failures in production

symptoms: want to reduce bundle size
@huggingface/transformers adds megabytes to the JS bundle
root_cause: The library bundles tokenizers and runtime glue. Most of the size is unavoidable, but tree-shakable subimports help.
fix: Import only what you use: import { pipeline } from '@huggingface/transformers'
Code-split: only load Transformers.js inside the worker, never on the initial route
If you use a single tokenizer, prefer pipeline() over pulling AutoTokenizer + AutoModel separately

symptoms: want offline-first behaviour
PWA must work without network
root_cause: Models are served via fetch and cached in IndexedDB; making the app a PWA gives full offline support after the first load.
fix: Register a service worker that precaches the JS shell
Don't try to precache the model files in the SW — let Transformers.js handle them in IndexedDB
Detect first-load and prompt the user to download the model before going offline

symptoms: want to show estimated cold-load time to users
model size shown but not user-friendly
root_cause: The progress_callback gives loaded/total bytes; converting to seconds requires measuring network speed.
fix: Track elapsed and loaded across the first few progress events to compute bytes/sec
Estimate remaining = (total - loaded) / bytes_per_sec
Display both percentage and a human-readable ETA

symptoms: want to know if WebGPU is available before loading a model
Safari users can't run the WebGPU build
root_cause: WebGPU is gated by `navigator.gpu`. Some browsers expose the global but `requestAdapter()` returns null on unsupported hardware.
fix: if (!('gpu' in navigator)) { device = 'wasm' }
const adapter = await navigator.gpu?.requestAdapter(); if (!adapter) device = 'wasm'
Display a 'GPU acceleration available' badge after a successful adapter request

symptoms: Error: failed to create webgpu device
WebGPU adapter request returned null on Linux/Firefox
root_cause: WebGPU is not enabled by default on every platform. Linux frequently needs explicit flags; Firefox ships WebGPU only behind a flag.
fix: Catch the error and fall back to { device: 'wasm' }
On Chrome Linux, ensure chrome://flags/#enable-unsafe-webgpu is on for end users in dev only — production must work without it
Set instructions in your UI: 'GPU not available, running on CPU' instead of failing silently

symptoms: very large model file from CDN times out
fetch aborted at 30s
root_cause: Some CDNs and proxies cap response duration. Transformers.js uses standard fetch; long downloads need correct cache headers and no proxy timeouts.
fix: Host weights on a CDN that supports range requests and long downloads (Hugging Face CDN, Cloudflare R2, Vercel Blob)
Avoid Vercel Edge Middleware for proxying model files; serve them as static assets instead
Pre-warm cache on a 'Download model' click so users opt in

symptoms: running multiple models concurrently
memory pressure with several pipelines
root_cause: Each pipeline holds its own session; loading more than one large model at once will OOM mobile devices.
fix: Serialise model use: dispose() the previous pipeline before loading a new one
On WebGPU, sessions hold GPU buffers — explicit dispose() is required
Keep small auxiliary models (VAD, tokenizers) hot, load big models on demand

symptoms: want to call a model with multiple inputs in one batch
single-sample API too slow
root_cause: Most pipelines accept arrays; the runtime batches into one ONNX graph run for far better throughput than N sequential calls.
fix: const out = await classifier(['a', 'b', 'c']) // one batch instead of three calls
Pad to the max length within the batch (the tokenizer does this automatically)
On WebGPU keep batch sizes modest (4-16) to avoid GPU memory spikes

symptoms: want to abort a running inference
user clicked Cancel mid-generation
root_cause: ONNX Runtime Web doesn't expose interrupt for an in-flight session.run(), but generation loops accept a stopping criteria you can flip.
fix: For text-generation, pass { generation_config: { ... }, stopping_criteria } and break the loop when a flag flips
For long audio split into chunks; cancel between chunks rather than mid-chunk
Reset the worker by terminating and recreating it as the absolute last resort

symptoms: tokenizer behaviour seems off
want to inspect tokens before generation
root_cause: AutoTokenizer is exposed and you can decode/encode independently of a model.
fix: import { AutoTokenizer } from '@huggingface/transformers'
const tok = await AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model)
const ids = tok.encode('hello world'); console.log(ids, tok.decode(ids))

symptoms: tokenizer 'Unknown padding strategy: longest' error
wrong padding token id
root_cause: Tokenizers fall back to model defaults; for custom batches you need to pass padding/truncation explicitly.
fix: tokenizer(texts, { padding: true, truncation: true, max_length: 128 })
If pad_token is undefined, set tok.pad_token = tok.eos_token before encoding
Confirm the resulting input_ids tensor has the same shape across batch entries

symptoms: want to use AutoModel directly
pipeline() abstraction is too high level
root_cause: AutoTokenizer + AutoModel give you direct ONNX session control for custom workflows.
fix: import { AutoTokenizer, AutoModel } from '@huggingface/transformers'
const [tok, model] = await Promise.all([AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(id), AutoModel.from_pretrained(id, { dtype: 'q8' })])
const inputs = await tok('hello'); const out = await model(inputs)

symptoms: want to inspect raw tensor outputs
need .data, .dims for custom math
root_cause: Pipeline outputs are post-processed JS objects; for raw tensors use AutoModel and read tensor.data (a typed array) and tensor.dims.
fix: const out = await model(inputs)
const logits = out.logits; console.log(logits.dims, logits.data.length)
Convert to a regular JS array with Array.from(logits.data) only for small tensors

symptoms: want consistent results across runs
non-deterministic generation outputs
root_cause: Sampling is stochastic by default. For reproducible runs disable sampling.
fix: Pass { do_sample: false } for greedy decoding
Pin temperature, top_k, top_p, and use repetition_penalty if needed
Don't expect bit-exact equality across browsers; floating-point implementations differ

symptoms: want to set max output length
model produces very long replies
root_cause: max_new_tokens caps the number of generated tokens regardless of input length; max_length caps total length including the prompt.
fix: Prefer max_new_tokens for chat-style apps: { max_new_tokens: 256 }
Use max_length only when you need to enforce a hard total context limit
Pair with stopping_criteria to break early on a stop string

symptoms: want temperature/top_p to feel like a chat model
outputs feel robotic
root_cause: Default greedy decoding is deterministic but bland. Sampling with temperature 0.7 + top_p 0.9 gives a chat-like feel for most LLMs.
fix: { do_sample: true, temperature: 0.7, top_p: 0.9, top_k: 0 }
Lower temperature for code or factual tasks (0.2-0.4)
Raise temperature for creative writing (0.9-1.1) but watch for incoherence

symptoms: RangeError: Invalid typed array length
generation aborts after a few tokens
root_cause: Allocating typed arrays beyond engine limits, often caused by oversized max_new_tokens or missing pad/eos token configuration.
fix: Cap max_new_tokens to a sane number (256 for chat, 1024 for long-form)
Ensure tok.eos_token_id is set on the generation config so the model can stop
Switch to a smaller dtype to free memory headroom

symptoms: want to display partial decoded text
TextStreamer prints to console only
root_cause: TextStreamer accepts a callback_function so you can route tokens anywhere — postMessage, React state, or DOM updates.
fix: new TextStreamer(tok, { skip_prompt: true, skip_special_tokens: true, callback_function: (txt) => self.postMessage({ type: 'token', txt }) })
On the UI side accumulate tokens with setText((t) => t + msg.txt)
Throttle DOM updates with requestAnimationFrame for very fast generation

symptoms: want to support multiple model variants from one UI
let users pick small/medium/large
root_cause: Centralise the catalog of model id + dtype combos and expose them in the UI; reload the worker when the user changes selection.
fix: const CATALOG = [{ id: 'onnx-community/whisper-tiny', dtype: 'q8' }, { id: 'onnx-community/whisper-base', dtype: 'q8' }, ...]
Persist the choice in localStorage; default to the smallest variant
Wipe IndexedDB only on user request, not on swap, so caches survive switches

symptoms: Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 403
gated model can't be downloaded in the browser
root_cause: Some Hub repos are gated and require a token to access. Browser apps can't ship a Hub token securely.
fix: Mirror the model to a non-gated repo you control if the licence allows
Self-host the converted ONNX files on your own CDN
Don't pass HF tokens from the browser; that leaks the credential

symptoms: want consistent fp16 behaviour across browsers
model precision differs between WebGPU and WASM
root_cause: WebGPU supports fp16 natively; WASM falls back to fp32 emulation for fp16 weights, which is slower and may differ numerically.
fix: Use { dtype: 'fp16', device: 'webgpu' } as a pair on capable hardware
Use { dtype: 'q8', device: 'wasm' } as the safe fallback on CPU
Don't ship fp16 to wasm in production; the runtime upcasts and you lose the size benefit

symptoms: want to release GPU memory
subsequent loads OOM the GPU
root_cause: WebGPU sessions hold device buffers until disposed. JS GC doesn't free them eagerly.
fix: await pipe.dispose() before reassigning the singleton
Wrap reload in try/finally to guarantee disposal even on error
On reload, also drop references to streamer and tokenizer caches

symptoms: want to test on Node
Transformers.js usable in Node for CI
root_cause: Transformers.js runs on Node.js too — useful for golden tests of model output without a browser.
fix: Install in Node and import the same package; the runtime auto-selects the Node binding for ONNX Runtime
Use sharp for image inputs, fs.readFileSync for audio
CI runs against q8 to keep tests fast and deterministic

symptoms: want Transformers.js logs to flow to a custom logger
console.log littered with 'Loading...'
root_cause: By default loggers go to console.log/warn/error. Override via env or by intercepting with your own console wrapper.
fix: Provide a progress_callback so the default logger isn't installed
Wrap console with a no-op for 'transformers' prefixed messages in production
Forward important events (model loaded, error) to your own observability layer

symptoms: need question answering on a passage
want extractive QA
root_cause: question-answering pipeline returns answer span + score from a context.
fix: const qa = await pipeline('question-answering', 'Xenova/distilbert-base-cased-distilled-squad')
const out = await qa('Where is Paris?', 'Paris is the capital of France.')
out -> { answer: 'France', score: 0.6, start: 25, end: 31 }

symptoms: need named-entity recognition
want to extract people/places/organisations
root_cause: token-classification pipeline returns spans for each entity with confidence scores.
fix: const ner = await pipeline('token-classification', 'Xenova/bert-base-NER')
const out = await ner('My name is Sarah and I live in London.', { ignore_labels: [] })
Use { aggregation_strategy: 'simple' } to merge subword tokens into entities

symptoms: need summarisation in the browser
want to compress an article
root_cause: summarization pipeline wraps seq2seq models like distilbart and bart-large-cnn.
fix: const summariser = await pipeline('summarization', 'Xenova/distilbart-cnn-6-6')
const out = await summariser(longText, { max_new_tokens: 120, min_new_tokens: 40 })
Chunk inputs longer than the model's max_position_embeddings

symptoms: need image segmentation
want to extract foreground from background
root_cause: image-segmentation supports semantic / panoptic models; for matting use background-removal models like RMBG.
fix: const segmenter = await pipeline('image-segmentation', 'Xenova/segformer-b0-finetuned-ade-512-512')
const out = await segmenter(imageUrl) // returns array of { mask, label, score }
For background removal use 'briaai/RMBG-1.4' (ONNX export)

symptoms: need depth estimation
want to build a 3D effect from a photo
root_cause: depth-estimation pipeline runs models like MiDaS and Depth Anything in the browser.
fix: const depth = await pipeline('depth-estimation', 'onnx-community/depth-anything-v2-small')
const { predicted_depth } = await depth(imageUrl)
Render predicted_depth to a canvas via grayscale mapping for visualisation

symptoms: want to share the same worker across pages
model reloads when navigating in a SPA
root_cause: Web Workers live as long as the page that created them; navigating between SPA routes that mount/unmount the worker re-downloads the model. SharedWorker survives across same-origin tabs.
fix: Hoist the worker creation to the app shell so SPA navigation doesn't tear it down
Use a SharedWorker if you need to share state across tabs of the same origin
In React keep the worker in a top-level provider with useRef + useEffect cleanup

symptoms: want to ship an offline demo
models embedded with the app
root_cause: For completely-offline distribution put the ONNX files into your static assets and point env.localModelPath at them.
fix: Place files under public/models/<org>/<model>/onnx/* (mirror the Hub layout)
env.allowLocalModels = true; env.allowRemoteModels = false; env.localModelPath = '/models/'
Test with the network panel offline-mode enabled to confirm no remote requests

symptoms: Astro/Remix build fails on import of @huggingface/transformers
import.meta.env not defined
root_cause: Some meta-frameworks treat the package as universal and try to import it on the server. Force it to client-only.
fix: Use the framework's client-only directive (Astro: client:only="react"; Remix: dynamic import inside useEffect)
Wrap imports with typeof window !== 'undefined' guards if you must keep them isomorphic
Move the import into a worker file that the framework already treats as a separate chunk

symptoms: want to print model metadata after load
verify which weights were used
root_cause: Loaded models expose .config and the tokenizer exposes .name — useful for diagnostics.
fix: console.log(pipe.model.config) // architecture-specific fields like d_model, vocab_size
console.log(pipe.tokenizer.name_or_path) // the resolved model id
Inspect ort session input/output shapes via pipe.model.session.inputNames/outputNames

symptoms: want to secure model files behind auth
block unauthenticated downloads
root_cause: If you self-host weights, cookie or signed-URL gating works because env.remoteHost obeys credentials when configured.
fix: Set env.remoteHost to your authenticated CDN URL
Use signed URLs (S3 presign / Vercel Blob signed) with short TTLs
Generate the URL server-side and pass it into the worker via postMessage on session start

symptoms: want to attribute model usage to a user
track inference per session
root_cause: All inference happens client-side; you have no server-side hook. Use client-side telemetry only.
fix: Send analytics events from the worker via postMessage and forward to your provider on the main thread
Don't post raw user inputs unless you obtain consent
Aggregate by anonymous session id, not user id

symptoms: need image-to-text captioning
want to describe an image
root_cause: image-to-text wraps vision-language models like BLIP and ViT-GPT2.
fix: const captioner = await pipeline('image-to-text', 'Xenova/vit-gpt2-image-captioning')
const out = await captioner(imageUrl)
out -> [{ generated_text: 'a cat sitting on a couch' }]

symptoms: need object detection
want bounding boxes for objects
root_cause: object-detection pipeline supports DETR and YOLOS models converted to ONNX.
fix: const detector = await pipeline('object-detection', 'Xenova/detr-resnet-50')
const out = await detector(imageUrl, { threshold: 0.9 })
Each result has { box: { xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax }, label, score }

symptoms: need zero-shot image classification
use CLIP-like models
root_cause: zero-shot-image-classification scores arbitrary text labels against an image using CLIP / SigLIP.
fix: const z = await pipeline('zero-shot-image-classification', 'Xenova/clip-vit-base-patch32')
const out = await z(imageUrl, ['a photo of a dog', 'a photo of a cat'])
Pass natural-language captions, not single words, for best accuracy

symptoms: warning: ONNX Runtime warning about unused initializers
noisy ort console messages
root_cause: ONNX Runtime emits informational logs by default. Silence them via env.backends.onnx.logSeverityLevel.
fix: env.backends.onnx.logSeverityLevel = 3 // 0 verbose, 1 info, 2 warning, 3 error, 4 fatal
Set during app startup, before pipeline() is called
Keep severity 2+ in development to surface real issues

symptoms: want to override how files are fetched
need a custom auth header on Hub requests
root_cause: Transformers.js exposes a customFetch hook on env that wraps every download. Use it to add headers, log, or rewrite URLs.
fix: env.customFetch = (url, init) => fetch(url, { ...init, headers: { ...init?.headers, 'X-My-Header': 'val' } })
Useful for proxying through your own server when CORS blocks direct Hub access
Avoid putting bearer tokens in this hook on the client; use signed URLs instead

symptoms: want to verify a model file's integrity
concerned about supply-chain tampering
root_cause: Hub files are immutable per revision; pin a revision and verify the SHA256 of the served file matches.
fix: Pass { revision: '<commit-sha>' } to pin
If hosting your own copies, compute a SHA-256 over the .onnx file and compare in env.customFetch
Store expected hashes in your build pipeline, not in the client

symptoms: want to chain multiple models
embed-then-classify or transcribe-then-translate
root_cause: Each pipeline can be awaited in series; share the worker so weights stay hot between stages.
fix: const [whisper, translator] = await Promise.all([pipeline('automatic-speech-recognition', ...), pipeline('translation', ...)])
const transcript = await whisper(audio)
const out = await translator(transcript.text, { src_lang: ..., tgt_lang: ... })

symptoms: want to keep models warm in IndexedDB across deploys
users re-download after every release
root_cause: Cache keys are URL-based; if you change env.remoteHost or env.localModelPath the cache is effectively reset.
fix: Keep model URLs stable across deploys
Don't append query strings or build hashes to ONNX files
If you must invalidate, do it explicitly via env.useBrowserCache = false on a one-off release

symptoms: preflight before shipping a Transformers.js feature
want to avoid common production gotchas
root_cause: A handful of recurring issues bite every team going to production: SSR, COOP/COEP, dtype, dispose(), bundler fallbacks, progress UX.
fix: Imports gated behind dynamic({ ssr: false }) or worker-only
Webpack/Vite configured with fs/path/crypto fallbacks
COOP+COEP headers set on the document for SharedArrayBuffer
Picked dtype + device combo and tested on a low-end device
pipeline disposed between model swaps
Progress UX shown for first cold load
Remote model URLs stable across deploys

symptoms: want to know which browsers support WebGPU
support matrix unclear
root_cause: WebGPU support varies: Chrome/Edge stable on Windows/macOS/ChromeOS, Safari 18+ on macOS/iOS 17+, Firefox behind a flag (Nightly stable). Linux often needs a flag.
fix: Treat WebGPU as progressive enhancement; always have a WASM fallback
Track caniuse.com/webgpu for current support
Test Safari 17 (no WebGPU) and Safari 18 (WebGPU on) as separate buckets

symptoms: want to migrate from @xenova/transformers to @huggingface/transformers
v2 to v3 upgrade
root_cause: v3 changed the import path, added the dtype + device options, and renamed quantized to dtype-based selection.
fix: Replace 'import { pipeline } from "@xenova/transformers"' with 'import { pipeline } from "@huggingface/transformers"'
Replace { quantized: true } with { dtype: 'q8' }
Audit env.* keys; some moved (e.g., backends.onnx.wasm.proxy is gone)

symptoms: Module not found when importing from worker
TypeError: Failed to construct 'Worker': type module is required
root_cause: Web Workers loading ESM code must be created with { type: 'module' }. Without it, ESM imports inside the worker file throw.
fix: new Worker(new URL('./worker.ts', import.meta.url), { type: 'module' })
Don't mix classic and module workers in the same project — pick one
If you need IE-era browsers, transpile the worker to a single file and drop type: 'module'

symptoms: want to set per-model latency budget
users complain about slow responses
root_cause: Browser inference latency depends on dtype, backend, and hardware. Set realistic targets and instrument the path.
fix: Wrap the pipeline call with performance.now() bookends and post the elapsed ms back to the UI
Track p50 and p95 separately; mobile has a long tail
Choose a smaller model variant if the budget can't be met on a target device class

symptoms: want to load a model from a URL string
have a custom hosted ONNX bundle
root_cause: Setting env.remoteHost + env.remotePathTemplate redirects the loader; you can also point env.localModelPath at any same-origin path.
fix: env.allowLocalModels = true; env.localModelPath = 'https://my.cdn/models/'
Mirror the Hub layout exactly: <localModelPath>/<org>/<model>/onnx/model.onnx
Pass the model id without https://: pipeline('text-classification', 'org/model')

symptoms: iOS Safari runs out of memory more aggressively than other browsers
page reloads silently on iOS
root_cause: iOS limits per-tab memory tightly; loading large models often triggers a silent reload. The fix is to use the smallest viable model + dtype and to avoid double-allocating on swap.
fix: Default iOS users to whisper-tiny + dtype q8 unless WebGPU is detected
Always dispose() before loading a new model
Avoid transferring large Float32Arrays to the worker — share via SharedArrayBuffer where possible

symptoms: want to measure model inference time
need throughput numbers
root_cause: performance.now() inside the worker captures the runtime cleanly without main-thread jitter.
fix: const t0 = performance.now(); const out = await pipe(input); const dt = performance.now() - t0
Run a warm-up pass first so JIT and shader compile costs don't pollute timings
Sample 30+ runs to compute p50/p95

symptoms: need to feed a Float32Array to a pipeline
have raw audio samples
root_cause: ASR pipelines accept Float32Array directly; samples must be mono and at the model's expected sample rate.
fix: Ensure samples is a Float32Array of mono audio at 16 kHz for Whisper
Pass directly: await transcriber(samples)
If the array is not Float32 (e.g., Float64), convert: new Float32Array(srcArray)

symptoms: creating a new pipeline per request
every onSubmit calls pipeline() afresh
root_cause: Each pipeline() call does network IO and ONNX session init; doing it per request wastes the IndexedDB cache and forces session re-creation.
fix: Memoise the pipeline as a module-scope singleton in the worker
Wrap with const getPipe = (() => { let p; return async () => p ??= pipeline(...); })()
Reload only when the user explicitly switches model variants

symptoms: running inference on the main thread
UI stalls during long generations
root_cause: The main thread shares time with rendering and input. Long sync inference blocks paint and produces an unresponsive page.
fix: Always run pipeline() inside a Web Worker for production
Communicate via postMessage with a discriminated union message protocol
Reserve the main thread for UI state, audio capture / playback, and worker lifecycle

symptoms: want a graceful first-run experience
users surprised by big download
root_cause: First run downloads hundreds of MB. Surprising users with a long load is a top reason for bounces.
fix: Show a 'Download model (NNN MB)' button so users opt-in
Display progress with file name and percentage from progress_callback
Mention that subsequent loads are instant and offline-capable

symptoms: Cypress / Playwright tests time out loading the model
headless browser slower than dev
root_cause: Headless Chromium can be slower at WebGPU and downloads weights with no cache between test runs.
fix: Persist a user data dir between tests so IndexedDB survives
Mock the pipeline in tests by intercepting env.customFetch and returning fixtures
Mark long tests as slow and bump per-test timeouts (60s+)

symptoms: need a fixed embedding dimension
build a vector search in the browser
root_cause: Sentence-transformers models on Transformers.js return mean-pooled normalised vectors; pick a model whose hidden_size matches your storage layout.
fix: all-MiniLM-L6-v2 -> 384 dim; bge-small-en -> 384; bge-base-en -> 768
Store as Float32Array in IndexedDB or a tiny in-memory matrix
Use cosine similarity = dot product when vectors are normalised

symptoms: want to keep model weights versioned with the app
self-hosting with stable URLs
root_cause: Stable URLs are critical for IndexedDB cache hits and reproducible deploys.
fix: Mirror the Hub layout under /models/<org>/<model>/onnx/*
Keep file names unchanged (model.onnx, model_q8.onnx, tokenizer.json, config.json)
Avoid query strings or hashed filenames; let the JS bundle hash but leave model files alone

symptoms: console error: 'Refused to apply style because its MIME type is wrong'
MIME type for .onnx or .json wrong
root_cause: Some static hosts default to text/plain for unknown extensions; ONNX Runtime expects application/octet-stream and tokenizer JSON files need application/json.
fix: Configure the host to send application/json for *.json and application/octet-stream for *.onnx
On Vercel use a vercel.json headers block targeting /models/* paths
On Cloudflare Pages add _headers with /models/* application/octet-stream

symptoms: want to limit what users can run
need server-side allowlist for models
root_cause: Because all execution is client-side you cannot enforce runtime allowlists; instead constrain at build time and through the server-issued URLs.
fix: Hardcode the small list of allowed model ids in the app
If you need rotation, fetch the catalog from your own API at startup and pass values into the worker
Avoid letting users supply arbitrary Hub ids in production

symptoms: Vite dev server loads model slowly with HMR refresh
every file save retriggers model load
root_cause: HMR remounts components and may re-create the worker, which re-runs pipeline(). The IndexedDB cache helps but session init still costs ~100-500ms.
fix: Move the worker creation outside the React tree so HMR doesn't remount it
Memoise heavy state with useState initialiser + useRef so re-renders don't re-do work
In dev, accept that the first save after a worker change will pay the session init cost

symptoms: want to compare two models on the same input
A/B test in the browser
root_cause: Run both pipelines (potentially in two workers) and average results. Don't share weights between them.
fix: Spawn worker A and worker B; load distinct models in each
Send the same input to both, await both responses
Display side-by-side with timing and confidence; let the user pick the winner

symptoms: want minimum confidence filtering
low-score predictions noisy
root_cause: Most pipelines return a score; threshold-filter on the client to hide spurious results.
fix: results.filter(r => r.score >= 0.6) for classification tasks
For object-detection use the threshold option (e.g., { threshold: 0.85 }) rather than post-filtering
Surface the threshold knob in the UI for power users

symptoms: want a quick reference of supported tasks
what can the pipeline do
root_cause: v3 supports a large set of tasks: text-classification, token-classification, question-answering, fill-mask, summarization, translation, text2text-generation, text-generation, zero-shot-classification, audio-classification, automatic-speech-recognition, text-to-audio, image-classification, image-segmentation, object-detection, zero-shot-object-detection, image-to-text, image-to-image, depth-estimation, feature-extraction, image-feature-extraction, zero-shot-image-classification, document-question-answering, image-to-text.
fix: Pick the task name that matches your problem
Each task has a default model; override with the second argument to pipeline()
Read the task page on the Hugging Face Transformers.js docs for input/output shapes

symptoms: want to host a Transformers.js audio app on Vercel
unsure how to fit audio ML in serverless
root_cause: Vercel Serverless Functions have a 50 MB deployment cap and 30 s Free Tier execution timeout. AI models are typically hundreds of MB and inference can take seconds, so models cannot run inside Functions. Vercel must serve only static assets while inference runs in the browser.
fix: Treat Vercel as a CDN/edge layer for static HTML/JS/CSS only
Run all inference in a Web Worker on the client
Download models from the Hugging Face CDN (or Vercel Blob) directly to the browser and cache in IndexedDB

symptoms: want a clean separation between UI thread and ML thread
audio app's UI freezes during transcription
root_cause: The recommended architecture is: Next.js UI Thread <-> Web Worker (Transformers.js + ONNX Runtime Web) -> WebGPU/WASM, with model storage in IndexedDB and audio capture via MediaDevices on the main thread.
fix: Main thread: handle UI, MediaRecorder/getUserMedia, playback, postMessage to/from the worker
Worker thread: own the pipeline singleton, all inference, and audio resampling
Send audio buffers as transferables; receive transcripts back as strings

symptoms: want recommended folder layout for a Next.js audio app
where do worker files live
root_cause: A clean layout for App Router puts UI in app/, the worker in workers/, and reusable client components in components/. Keep the worker out of the SSR graph.
fix: app/page.tsx imports a client component dynamically with { ssr: false }
components/AudioInterface.tsx is the 'use client' shell that owns the worker
workers/audio.worker.ts contains all Transformers.js code

symptoms: Next.js audio app: Module not found 'fs' / 'path' / 'crypto' on client build
ONNX Runtime Web pulls in Node-only modules
root_cause: ONNX Runtime Web's optional Node code paths show up in the client bundle. Without the webpack fallback the build fails.
fix: In next.config.mjs add a webpack hook that disables fs/path/crypto on the client
if (!isServer) config.resolve.fallback = { ...config.resolve.fallback, fs: false, path: false, crypto: false }
Don't disable them on the server bundle

symptoms: want SSR turned off for the audio interface
navigator.mediaDevices not available on server
root_cause: AudioInterface relies on browser-only APIs: navigator.mediaDevices, window, Web Workers, WebGPU. Loading it via next/dynamic with ssr: false ensures it never runs on Vercel's servers.
fix: import dynamic from 'next/dynamic'
const AudioInterface = dynamic(() => import('@/components/AudioInterface'), { ssr: false })
Use it inside any server or client page; React will only render it on the client

symptoms: need a minimal AudioInterface component
want a record-and-transcribe button
root_cause: AudioInterface owns the worker, captures audio with getUserMedia + MediaRecorder, sends Float32Array buffers to the worker, and renders the streamed transcript.
fix: 'use client' at the top
useEffect to create the worker once: const worker = useRef<Worker>()
On record click: get a stream, record, decode to Float32Array @16 kHz mono, postMessage to worker
onmessage handler appends streamed tokens to a transcript state

symptoms: need a minimal audio.worker.ts for Whisper
where to put the pipeline singleton
root_cause: The worker file imports Transformers.js, holds the pipeline as a module-scope singleton, and listens for { type: 'transcribe', audio } messages.
fix: import { pipeline } from '@huggingface/transformers'
let pipe; async function getPipe() { return pipe ??= pipeline('automatic-speech-recognition', 'onnx-community/whisper-base', { dtype: 'q8' }) }
self.onmessage = async (e) => { if (e.data.type === 'transcribe') { const p = await getPipe(); const out = await p(e.data.audio); self.postMessage({ type: 'done', text: out.text }) } }

symptoms: need to capture microphone input
getUserMedia recipe
root_cause: navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: true }) returns a MediaStream you can pipe into MediaRecorder or an AudioContext.
fix: const stream = await navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: { channelCount: 1, sampleRate: 16000 } })
Browsers may ignore sampleRate; resample yourself with OfflineAudioContext to be safe
Stop tracks when done: stream.getTracks().forEach(t => t.stop())

symptoms: Permission denied for microphone on production
getUserMedia rejects with NotAllowedError
root_cause: Microphone access requires a secure context (HTTPS). Vercel deploys are HTTPS by default; localhost is also a secure context. Custom domains over HTTP will fail.
fix: Always serve over HTTPS (Vercel does this automatically for *.vercel.app and verified custom domains)
Handle the rejection: catch (e) { showError('Allow microphone access in browser settings') }
Test on localhost or 127.0.0.1 in dev — not on a LAN IP, which isn't a secure context

symptoms: need to record audio to a Blob
MediaRecorder usage
root_cause: MediaRecorder captures a stream into chunks; assemble them into a Blob and decode for inference.
fix: const recorder = new MediaRecorder(stream, { mimeType: 'audio/webm;codecs=opus' })
const chunks = []; recorder.ondataavailable = (e) => chunks.push(e.data); recorder.onstop = () => process(new Blob(chunks))
Choose webm/opus on Chromium; Safari may need audio/mp4 — feature-detect with MediaRecorder.isTypeSupported

symptoms: need to convert a recorded Blob to Float32 PCM at 16 kHz mono
Whisper expects 16 kHz
root_cause: Whisper and most ASR models in Transformers.js take Float32Array @ 16 kHz mono. Browsers usually record at 44.1/48 kHz; an OfflineAudioContext does the resample.
fix: const arrayBuffer = await blob.arrayBuffer()
const ac = new AudioContext(); const audio = await ac.decodeAudioData(arrayBuffer)
const off = new OfflineAudioContext(1, Math.ceil(audio.duration * 16000), 16000)
const src = off.createBufferSource(); src.buffer = audio; src.connect(off.destination); src.start(0)
const rendered = await off.startRendering(); const f32 = rendered.getChannelData(0)

symptoms: audio is stereo and giving worse transcription
two-channel input
root_cause: Whisper is a mono model. Sending only one channel discards spatial info but is faster and matches training distribution.
fix: Take channel 0: rendered.getChannelData(0)
Or mix-down: const mix = new Float32Array(L); for (let i=0; i<L; i++) mix[i] = (left[i] + right[i]) * 0.5
Set OfflineAudioContext numberOfChannels = 1 to render mono directly

symptoms: audio decodeAudioData is unreliable on Safari for webm
decodeAudioData throws on iOS
root_cause: Safari doesn't decode Opus inside webm reliably. MediaRecorder defaults differ between Chromium and Safari.
fix: Detect MediaRecorder.isTypeSupported('audio/webm;codecs=opus') vs 'audio/mp4'
Fall back to AudioWorklet capture if MediaRecorder Opus is unavailable
On iOS use audio/mp4 (AAC) and accept the slightly larger file size

symptoms: want to capture audio without MediaRecorder
need streaming PCM frames for live transcription
root_cause: AudioWorklet runs in the audio thread and emits frames you can ship to the worker for live, low-latency transcription.
fix: Create an AudioWorkletProcessor that posts incoming frames as Float32Arrays
On the main thread, accumulate a sliding window (e.g., 5 s) and post to the worker for chunked transcription
Combine with a VAD to send only voiced chunks

symptoms: want to use Whisper for transcription
task name and model id
root_cause: automatic-speech-recognition is the task; pick a Whisper variant from onnx-community based on size/speed needs.
fix: const transcriber = await pipeline('automatic-speech-recognition', 'onnx-community/whisper-base', { dtype: 'q8' })
For the smallest cold start: 'onnx-community/whisper-tiny' (~40 MB q8)
For best quality on capable devices: 'onnx-community/whisper-large-v3-turbo' with WebGPU

symptoms: want to transcribe audio longer than 30 seconds
Whisper expects 30 s windows
root_cause: Whisper accepts a 30 s mel spectrogram. Transformers.js handles long-form via chunk_length_s and stride_length_s; the pipeline slices and stitches automatically.
fix: await transcriber(audio, { chunk_length_s: 30, stride_length_s: 5, return_timestamps: true })
Stride overlap improves boundary stitching
For very long files, send 1-2 minute slices to the worker so progress can be reported per slice

symptoms: want word-level timestamps from Whisper
render karaoke-style highlight
root_cause: return_timestamps: 'word' returns per-token times; Transformers.js exposes them in the chunks array.
fix: await transcriber(audio, { return_timestamps: 'word', chunk_length_s: 30, stride_length_s: 5 })
Output shape: { text, chunks: [{ text, timestamp: [start, end] }] }
Use the timestamps to drive a visual progress indicator on a waveform

symptoms: want multilingual transcription
non-English audio
root_cause: Whisper-multilingual variants accept a language code; pass it via the language option.
fix: await transcriber(audio, { language: 'french', task: 'transcribe' })
Use task: 'translate' to get an English translation directly
Distil-whisper variants are English-only; check the model card before passing language

symptoms: want fastest Whisper variant
real-time transcription
root_cause: Distil-whisper and whisper-large-v3-turbo are tuned for speed. Combined with WebGPU + fp16/q4, real-time is feasible on capable laptops.
fix: On WebGPU: 'onnx-community/whisper-large-v3-turbo' with { dtype: 'q4', device: 'webgpu' }
On WASM: 'onnx-community/whisper-base' with { dtype: 'q8' }
Measure RTF (real-time factor): inference_time / audio_duration; aim for <1.0

symptoms: want voice activity detection before sending to Whisper
skip silence segments
root_cause: Silero VAD via Transformers.js (or onnxruntime-web directly) detects voiced segments; only run Whisper on those segments to save compute.
fix: Run a frame-by-frame VAD on the audio stream (e.g., 30 ms frames at 16 kHz)
Buffer voiced regions; flush to Whisper after a configurable silence timeout (e.g., 800 ms)
This pattern is typical of streaming ASR apps and dramatically reduces compute

symptoms: need text-to-speech in the browser
want to play synthesised speech
root_cause: text-to-speech tasks run with SpeechT5, MMS-TTS, or Kokoro; output a Float32Array waveform you can play via Web Audio.
fix: const tts = await pipeline('text-to-speech', 'Xenova/speecht5_tts', { quantized: false })
const out = await tts('Hello world', { speaker_embeddings: speakerEmb })
Convert out.audio to an AudioBuffer and play through an AudioBufferSourceNode

symptoms: need to play TTS output in the browser
Float32Array to playback
root_cause: Wrap the Float32 PCM in an AudioBuffer at the model's sample rate (commonly 16 kHz for SpeechT5 / MMS-TTS, 24 kHz for Kokoro) and start a buffer source.
fix: const ac = new AudioContext()
const buf = ac.createBuffer(1, audio.length, sampleRate); buf.copyToChannel(audio, 0)
const src = ac.createBufferSource(); src.buffer = buf; src.connect(ac.destination); src.start()

symptoms: models hosted on Hugging Face are slow to download from some regions
want a regional CDN
root_cause: Hugging Face fronts model files via Cloudflare. From most regions speeds are good; if you have specific geography concerns mirror to your own CDN or to Vercel Blob.
fix: Mirror models to Vercel Blob and serve via @vercel/blob signed URLs
Update env.remoteHost to point at your blob domain
Set long cache headers on the blob assets so browsers cache aggressively after first hit

symptoms: want to host model files on Vercel Blob
Vercel Blob doesn't preserve the Hub layout
root_cause: Vercel Blob stores arbitrary keys; you control the path. Replicate the Hub structure exactly under your blob root for env.remoteHost to resolve files.
fix: Upload to keys like models/onnx-community/whisper-base/onnx/encoder_model.onnx
Set env.remoteHost = 'https://<store>.public.blob.vercel-storage.com/models/'
env.remotePathTemplate = '{model}/resolve/{revision}/'
Issue: Whisper has multiple ONNX files (encoder, decoder, decoder_with_past); upload all of them

symptoms: Vercel Serverless deploy fails: 'Function size exceeds 50 MB'
tried to ship Transformers.js inside an API route
root_cause: Vercel Functions cap at 50 MB unzipped (250 MB on Pro). Transformers.js bundled with weights blows the limit instantly. The whole point of the browser-first architecture is to avoid running the model server-side.
fix: Don't import @huggingface/transformers in any file under app/api/* or pages/api/*
Move all ML code into a Web Worker that the browser fetches as a static asset
Run inference on the client; serverless stays for things like CSRF, auth, and metadata

symptoms: Vercel Serverless function timed out at 30 s
tried to transcribe a long audio in an API route
root_cause: Free Tier max duration is 10 s for Edge and 10/60 s for Node functions depending on plan; Pro maxDuration is 300 s. ML inference with cold starts easily exceeds these.
fix: Don't put inference behind a serverless route — it can't reasonably finish
If you must run server-side inference for some reason, use a long-running runtime (a separate VM or container) and keep Vercel for the UI
For 99% of audio apps, do the inference in the browser and skip server-side ML entirely

symptoms: want to set COOP/COEP headers on Vercel for SharedArrayBuffer
WASM threads disabled in production
root_cause: Vercel doesn't send cross-origin isolation headers by default. Use vercel.json (or next.config.mjs headers()) to add them.
fix: In next.config.mjs export async headers() that returns [{ source: '/(.*)', headers: [{ key: 'Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy', value: 'same-origin' }, { key: 'Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy', value: 'require-corp' }] }]
Verify with self.crossOriginIsolated === true in the production browser
Beware: COEP can break third-party iframes and images that don't send CORP

symptoms: external images fail after enabling COEP
third-party iframes blocked
root_cause: COEP: require-corp blocks any subresource that doesn't send Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy or CORS headers. Many third-party assets don't.
fix: Use credentialless mode where supported: COEP: credentialless (Chrome 96+)
Self-host critical third-party images so you control headers
Audit iframes; many third-party widgets break and need replacement

symptoms: Vercel Edge Middleware tries to inspect /api/mcp or model files and times out
middleware adds latency to model fetches
root_cause: Edge Middleware runs on every request matching its matcher. If the matcher is too broad it intercepts model file fetches and adds a per-request cost.
fix: Restrict middleware matcher to UI routes; exclude /models, /onnx, and any blob path
Keep middleware tiny — model fetches must be unmediated
Use vercel.json to set headers instead of middleware where possible

symptoms: want best Vercel region for an audio app
users complain about slow first load
root_cause: Static assets ship from Vercel's global CDN; only Functions are region-pinned. Static-only audio apps have minimal regional sensitivity.
fix: Don't pin a region in vercel.json unless you also have Functions
Let Vercel's edge serve static assets globally
If you mirror models in your own bucket, place them in a multi-region store

symptoms: want minimum scaffolding to host audio app on Vercel
what files to commit
root_cause: A working audio app on Vercel needs: app/page.tsx (server component), components/AudioInterface.tsx (client component, dynamic), workers/audio.worker.ts, next.config.mjs with webpack fallbacks + headers().
fix: next.config.mjs sets webpack fallback for fs/path/crypto and a headers() block for COOP/COEP
app/layout.tsx is the standard root layout
app/page.tsx renders the dynamic AudioInterface
components/AudioInterface.tsx ('use client') owns the worker and capture
workers/audio.worker.ts holds the pipeline and inference

symptoms: want first paint to be fast
audio model bytes shouldn't block FCP
root_cause: Model files are 40-500+ MB. Don't auto-load them on first paint. Trigger the download from a user action (Record / Try button) so initial paint stays light.
fix: Show the empty UI shell immediately; gate model load behind a click
Provide a 'Download speech model (NN MB)' CTA
Once loaded, persist that the model is cached and skip the CTA on return visits

symptoms: want to detect if user has a cached model
skip Download CTA on return visits
root_cause: IndexedDB exposes the cache; query for the model file key to check if it's already downloaded.
fix: Open 'transformers-cache' DB and check whether the expected key exists
Or attempt a small fetch with cache-only semantics and fall back to the CTA
Persist a localStorage flag once a successful first-load completes; cheap and reliable

symptoms: need to stream Whisper output token-by-token
want live partial transcripts
root_cause: Whisper exposes a callback_function via TextStreamer; tokens are emitted during decoding so partials can stream to the UI.
fix: Create a TextStreamer with skip_prompt: true and route tokens via postMessage to the UI
Append to a transcript buffer; rerender at requestAnimationFrame cadence
On chunk_length_s boundaries, mark a hard split in the UI to indicate a stable partial

symptoms: want to transcribe an uploaded audio file
support drag-and-drop
root_cause: File uploads work the same as MediaRecorder output: read as ArrayBuffer, decode via AudioContext, resample, send to worker.
fix: Accept files via <input type='file' accept='audio/*'>
Use the same decode + resample helper as the recorder path
Cap file size in the UI (e.g., 30 MB) and warn users about long transcription times

symptoms: want to support both record and upload flows
DRY between paths
root_cause: Both produce a Float32Array; share the post-decode pipeline so the worker receives one canonical shape.
fix: Define const toMonoFloat32At16k = async (input: Blob | File): Promise<Float32Array> => { ... }
Both record and upload buttons call this helper before postMessage
Worker stays simple: { type: 'transcribe', audio: Float32Array }

symptoms: users record very long audio and run out of memory
browser tab crashes after 30+ minutes
root_cause: Holding a 30+ minute Float32Array in memory doubles when posted to the worker (clone). Switch to streaming chunks rather than one giant buffer.
fix: Cut audio at chunk boundaries (e.g., every 60 s) and post each chunk separately
Use transferables ([buffer.buffer]) so each post is zero-copy
Append per-chunk results into the final transcript on the UI

symptoms: want a waveform visualisation while recording
show audio levels
root_cause: AnalyserNode in the Web Audio API exposes time-domain and frequency-domain data; render to a canvas at requestAnimationFrame.
fix: const ac = new AudioContext(); const src = ac.createMediaStreamSource(stream); const analyser = ac.createAnalyser(); src.connect(analyser)
Inside rAF: analyser.getFloatTimeDomainData(arr) and draw bars or a line
Stop the loop and disconnect when recording ends to free CPU

symptoms: want to handle microphone permission denied gracefully
user blocks mic
root_cause: getUserMedia rejects with NotAllowedError or SecurityError; the UI must guide users to re-enable in browser settings.
fix: Catch the rejection and present a permission-request UI
Detect navigator.permissions.query({ name: 'microphone' }) state to know if it's denied vs prompt
Provide a fallback to file-upload mode when mic isn't available

symptoms: model worker fails to start on first load
error before pipeline returns
root_cause: Model load can fail for many reasons: 404, network blip, dtype not supported. Provide good error messages and a retry path.
fix: Wrap getPipe() in try/catch and post { type: 'error', message } back to UI
Display a Retry button that re-invokes the load
Distinguish recoverable (network) from unrecoverable (404 / dtype) failures and show different copy

symptoms: want chunked progressive transcription UI
show running transcript as audio is processed
root_cause: Slice the buffer into N-second pieces and transcribe sequentially; the UI updates as each chunk completes for an apparent live feel.
fix: Slice into 30s chunks with a 5s overlap
For each chunk, postMessage to the worker and await the response before sending the next
Append text to the running transcript with a fade-in animation per chunk

symptoms: want lowest cold-start cost on Vercel
users land and bounce before model loads
root_cause: Cold start time is dominated by model download. Pick the smallest model + dtype that meets accuracy and load on demand.
fix: Default to whisper-tiny + dtype 'q8' for the bounce-tolerant first impression
Offer a 'better quality' upgrade that loads whisper-base only on user request
Persist the choice and skip the choice on return visits

symptoms: want to maximise quality on capable hardware
use bigger model when WebGPU available
root_cause: Conditional model loading lets you opt the user up to a stronger model when the GPU is available.
fix: if (hasWebGPU) load whisper-large-v3-turbo with { dtype: 'q4', device: 'webgpu' }
else load whisper-base with { dtype: 'q8', device: 'wasm' }
Tell the user which model is in use so quality differences are explained

symptoms: audio samples have wrong dtype going into Whisper
RangeError: Wrong dtype 'float64'
root_cause: Whisper expects Float32. AudioBuffer.getChannelData returns Float32Array but if you copy via Array.from or use Float64Array elsewhere it breaks.
fix: Always coerce: const f32 = audio instanceof Float32Array ? audio : new Float32Array(audio)
Avoid Array.from on PCM data — it makes a regular Array, which doesn't match
Verify: console.log(f32.constructor.name, f32.length)

symptoms: audio sample rate is 44100 and Whisper output is gibberish
model trained on 16kHz
root_cause: Whisper's mel filterbank expects 16 kHz; sending 44.1 kHz raw samples produces nonsense because the time axis is wrong by ~2.75x.
fix: Resample with OfflineAudioContext to 16000 before sending
Confirm rendered.sampleRate === 16000 after rendering
If using AudioWorklet streaming, downsample frame-by-frame with a 4-tap polyphase filter

symptoms: want consistent recording volume
transcription quality varies by mic distance
root_cause: Auto-gain helps consistency. getUserMedia accepts constraints to enable browser-side AGC, echo cancellation, and noise suppression.
fix: navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: { autoGainControl: true, echoCancellation: true, noiseSuppression: true, channelCount: 1 } })
Constraints are advisory; some browsers/devices ignore them
Ask users to position the mic 6-10 inches away in onboarding copy

symptoms: want to speed up transcription with WebGPU
see WebGPU benefit on Whisper
root_cause: WebGPU drastically accelerates Whisper encoder/decoder compared to WASM SIMD. Use { device: 'webgpu' } and pick a fp16 / q4 dtype tuned for GPU.
fix: await pipeline('automatic-speech-recognition', 'onnx-community/whisper-base', { device: 'webgpu', dtype: { encoder_model: 'fp16', decoder_model_merged: 'q4' } })
Benchmark: WebGPU on a recent laptop gets 5-10x WASM
Always feature-detect; fall back to WASM on failure

symptoms: Whisper-base on WASM SIMD takes 25s for 30s of audio
RTF ~0.83 on a mid-range laptop
root_cause: WASM SIMD threaded with COOP/COEP enabled can hit RTF ~0.8 on whisper-base q8. Without threads it's 2-3x slower.
fix: Verify cross-origin isolation (self.crossOriginIsolated === true)
Set env.backends.onnx.wasm.numThreads to navigator.hardwareConcurrency / 2
Switch to whisper-tiny on lower-end devices to keep RTF under 1.0

symptoms: Whisper-base on WebGPU runs at RTF ~0.1
10x faster than WASM
root_cause: WebGPU with fp16 encoder + q4 decoder is the fastest combo for Whisper-base today; 30s of audio transcribes in ~3s on a recent dGPU.
fix: Use { device: 'webgpu', dtype: { encoder_model: 'fp16', decoder_model_merged: 'q4' } }
Track GPU load on the Performance panel
WebGPU is browser/OS dependent; always provide WASM fallback

symptoms: want to keep audio data private
users worry about cloud upload
root_cause: Browser-only inference is a privacy win — audio never leaves the device. Make the privacy story explicit in the UI to build trust.
fix: Display a 'Your audio never leaves your device' note prominently
Avoid analytics that capture transcript content; only emit aggregate metrics
Use COEP: require-corp + a tight CSP to prevent third-party script exfiltration

symptoms: want to harden CSP for the audio app
third-party trackers leak audio app data
root_cause: Transformers.js loads code from your origin and weights from huggingface.co or a configured CDN. A tight CSP plus connect-src allowlist keeps everything else out.
fix: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'; connect-src 'self' https://huggingface.co https://*.huggingface.co
If you mirror weights to your own bucket, add that origin to connect-src
wasm-unsafe-eval is required for ONNX Runtime Web

symptoms: page reloads silently in the middle of transcription
iOS aborts long workers
root_cause: iOS aggressively reclaims memory and may suspend or terminate Web Workers on backgrounded tabs. Background tab behaviour also varies by browser.
fix: Use Page Visibility API to pause inference when document.hidden
Show a 'Keep this tab visible while transcribing' note in the UI
Don't expect long jobs to complete in a background tab on mobile

symptoms: want to allow downloading transcript
user wants TXT or SRT export
root_cause: Once Whisper returns the transcript with timestamps, build a Blob and trigger a download with a hidden anchor.
fix: TXT: const blob = new Blob([transcript.text], { type: 'text/plain' })

SRT: format chunks with timestamps as 'idx
00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:05,000
text

'
Trigger: const a = document.createElement('a'); a.href = URL.createObjectURL(blob); a.download = 'transcript.srt'; a.click()

symptoms: want to translate audio directly
user records French and wants English text
root_cause: Whisper's task option supports 'translate' which always emits English. Bypasses a separate translation pipeline for many use cases.
fix: await transcriber(audio, { task: 'translate', language: 'french' })
Output is English regardless of input language
For non-English targets, transcribe then run a separate translation pipeline

symptoms: Whisper hallucinates lyrics or repeated phrases on silent audio
transcript invents content
root_cause: Whisper is known to hallucinate on silent or noisy segments. Run a VAD upstream to skip silence, and use no_speech_threshold to filter low-confidence outputs.
fix: Pre-filter audio with Silero VAD; only run Whisper on voiced regions
Pass { no_speech_threshold: 0.6 } to suppress likely hallucinations
On long files use stride_length_s + chunk_length_s with overlap to stitch boundaries cleanly

symptoms: want to measure end-to-end latency for live ASR
user perceives lag
root_cause: End-to-end latency = audio capture frame size + post to worker + inference + post back + render. Each stage matters; instrument them all.
fix: Tag each message with a t_emit timestamp; subtract on receipt for inference + transport time
Measure capture frame size separately (smaller frames = lower latency, more overhead)
Aim for sub-300 ms end-to-end on whisper-tiny WebGPU

symptoms: want to fall back gracefully when Whisper download fails
offer 'try again later' message
root_cause: Network failures during a multi-hundred-MB model download are common. Provide a clear retry-with-backoff loop and avoid silent stalls.
fix: Wrap pipeline() in a retry loop with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, max 3 retries)
Show 'Retrying download…' state during retries
On final failure, offer an explicit Try Again button rather than auto-retrying

symptoms: want to combine VAD + Whisper for streaming live transcription
build a real-time captioning UI
root_cause: Pattern: AudioWorklet emits frames -> ring buffer -> VAD scores frames -> on speech start buffer; on speech end (silence > 800ms) flush to Whisper -> render partial.
fix: AudioWorkletProcessor posts 16 kHz mono Float32 frames to the main thread
Main thread runs VAD per frame; collects voiced frames into a buffer
On end-of-utterance (silence threshold), postMessage to Whisper worker
Render the returned text to the captions UI

symptoms: want fewer but stable VAD chunks
Whisper called too often per utterance
root_cause: VAD parameters control how aggressive end-of-speech detection is. Tune them to balance latency vs over-segmentation.
fix: Silence threshold: 800-1200 ms gives natural utterance boundaries
Min speech duration: 300 ms filters out clicks
Pre-padding 200 ms before voiced start avoids cutting first phoneme

symptoms: want to log only errors in production audio app
Whisper progress logs flooding logs
root_cause: Setting env.backends.onnx.logSeverityLevel and disabling default progress callbacks keeps production console quiet.
fix: env.backends.onnx.logSeverityLevel = 3 // error and above
Always pass an explicit progress_callback so the default logger is replaced
Forward only fatal errors to your own logging service

symptoms: Vercel build succeeds but runtime errors with 'window is not defined'
module-level pipeline() crashed during prerender
root_cause: Calling pipeline() at module top level in any file imported by an SSR-rendered page runs the call on Vercel's Node runtime, where window is undefined.
fix: Never call pipeline() at module top level — only inside a worker or a client-side useEffect
Confirm the only import of @huggingface/transformers is inside workers/audio.worker.ts
Use grep to verify no app/* or components/* file imports the package directly

symptoms: want to keep the Vercel deployment small
build artifact too large
root_cause: Static-only audio apps deploy small JS bundles; the issue is when ONNX wasm files or models accidentally land in the deployment.
fix: Don't copy ort wasm to public/ — leave them on a CDN via env.backends.onnx.wasm.wasmPaths
If you must self-host wasm, copy only the SIMD threaded variant (~8 MB)
Never commit ONNX models into the repo; load from HF or Vercel Blob

symptoms: ort-wasm-simd-threaded.jsep.wasm 404 in production
ONNX Runtime Web wasm files missing on Vercel
root_cause: The default wasmPaths points to a relative path that may not be served by the static host. You must either copy the files to a public folder or point at the unpkg/jsdelivr CDN.
fix: CDN approach: env.backends.onnx.wasm.wasmPaths = `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/onnxruntime-web@<exact-version>/dist/`
Self-host approach: copy node_modules/onnxruntime-web/dist/*.wasm to public/onnx and set wasmPaths = '/onnx/'
Pin the version in package.json so the CDN URL stays valid across deploys

symptoms: want to avoid worker pool churn on every page navigation
audio worker recreated on each route change
root_cause: Mount the worker high in the React tree (e.g., inside the root layout or a client provider) so SPA navigation between routes leaves it intact.
fix: Create a WorkerProvider client component and place it in app/layout.tsx (still server component-friendly via children)
Provide the worker instance through React context
Consumer components subscribe via useContext and don't manage worker lifecycle

symptoms: want a useTranscription React hook
encapsulate worker + state
root_cause: Wrap the worker boilerplate in a custom hook so any component can transcribe with three lines.
fix: function useTranscription() { const w = useContext(WorkerContext); const [text, setText] = useState(''); useEffect(() => { const h = (e) => e.data.type === 'token' && setText(t => t + e.data.txt); w.addEventListener('message', h); return () => w.removeEventListener('message', h) }, [w]); return { transcribe: (audio) => w.postMessage({ type: 'transcribe', audio }, [audio.buffer]), text } }
Component: const { transcribe, text } = useTranscription()
Resets text on a new transcribe call by setting setText('') first

symptoms: vercel.json headers not applied
COOP/COEP missing in production
root_cause: vercel.json headers apply only when the file is at the project root; missing or malformed JSON silently disables them.
fix: vercel.json at repo root with: { "headers": [{ "source": "/(.*)", "headers": [{ "key": "Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy", "value": "same-origin" }, { "key": "Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy", "value": "require-corp" }] }] }
Or move headers into next.config.mjs export async headers() — Next.js takes precedence
Verify with curl -I https://my.app and check the response headers

symptoms: want to A/B test small vs large Whisper
decide which to ship by default
root_cause: Different users have different network and hardware. Measure before committing to a default.
fix: Bucket users by hasWebGPU + hardwareConcurrency
Measure cold load + RTF + transcript quality (e.g., word error rate vs ground truth)
Default the smallest viable variant per bucket; let power users opt up

symptoms: want to comply with GDPR / sensitive audio policies
legal asks where audio is processed
root_cause: Browser-only inference means audio bytes never leave the user's device. This dramatically simplifies privacy and compliance posture.
fix: Document the architecture: 'audio processed locally in your browser, never uploaded'
Don't add server-side analytics that capture transcript content
Make weight downloads optional and show their size and origin

symptoms: want to play short audio cues during recording
start/stop sound effects
root_cause: Web Audio API plays decoded audio buffers; pre-load short PCM/WAV files into AudioBuffers at app start.
