fix: const ac = new AudioContext(); const buf = await ac.decodeAudioData(await (await fetch('/sfx/start.wav')).arrayBuffer())
Play: const src = ac.createBufferSource(); src.buffer = buf; src.connect(ac.destination); src.start()
iOS requires a user-gesture-triggered ac.resume() before any audio plays

symptoms: AudioContext is suspended on iOS until first tap
Web Audio silent on iPhone
root_cause: iOS only resumes AudioContext after a user gesture (touchend/click). Creating it before that leaves it suspended.
fix: Lazy-create the AudioContext inside an onClick handler
Or call ac.resume() inside the first user gesture
Detect: if (ac.state !== 'running') await ac.resume()

symptoms: want to render a live audio waveform while recording
show recording activity in the UI
root_cause: Connect getUserMedia stream to an AnalyserNode, then animate a canvas drawing the time-domain data each frame.
fix: const src = ac.createMediaStreamSource(stream); const an = ac.createAnalyser(); an.fftSize = 1024; src.connect(an)
rAF loop: an.getFloatTimeDomainData(buf); draw(buf) on the canvas
Stop the loop on recording end and disconnect to free CPU

symptoms: want to show input audio level (VU meter)
indicate microphone is picking up sound
root_cause: Compute RMS or peak from the time-domain buffer to drive a level meter; this is much cheaper than a full waveform.
fix: rms = sqrt(mean(samples^2)); db = 20 * log10(max(rms, 1e-6))
Map [-60dB, 0dB] to [0%, 100%] for a meter
Update at ~30 Hz for smoothness without overdrawing

symptoms: want stable model URLs across releases for cache hits
cache invalidates after every deploy
root_cause: Transformers.js keys IndexedDB entries by URL. Build hashes appended to model paths invalidate the cache on every deploy.
fix: Don't hash model URLs; only hash JS/CSS bundles
If you must rotate weights, do it explicitly via a revision pin so users opt into the redownload
Mirror to immutable storage (Vercel Blob with deterministic keys) to lock URLs

symptoms: want to limit deployment cost on Vercel for an audio app
free-tier safety
root_cause: Static-only deployments are essentially free on Vercel. Avoid serverless functions and edge middleware for the audio path entirely; only use them for non-ML concerns.
fix: Don't put inference behind /api/* — keep all ML in the browser
Use Edge Middleware sparingly (per-request cost)
Audit Vercel Analytics for unexpected function invocations

symptoms: want to attach metadata to a transcript (start time, model used)
store sessions for replay
root_cause: Wrap each transcript in a session object with model id, dtype, device, started_at, ended_at, and the chunks; persist to IndexedDB or a server.
fix: Define type Session = { id: string; modelId: string; dtype: string; device: string; chunks: Chunk[]; startedAt: number; endedAt: number }
Persist via IndexedDB on session end
Avoid uploading raw audio; if you must persist, store transcripts only

symptoms: want to verify mic permission before showing record button
avoid permission prompt mid-flow
root_cause: navigator.permissions.query lets you read microphone state without prompting.
fix: const status = await navigator.permissions.query({ name: 'microphone' })
If status.state === 'denied' show an instruction to re-enable in browser settings
Listen to status.onchange to refresh UI when the user grants permission

symptoms: want to show different UI based on device class
low-end mobile gets simpler UI
root_cause: Combine navigator.deviceMemory, hardwareConcurrency, and a WebGPU probe to estimate device class for graceful degradation.
fix: score = (deviceMemory ?? 4) + (hardwareConcurrency ?? 4) + (hasWebGPU ? 4 : 0)
score < 6: whisper-tiny + small UI
score >= 12: whisper-base + full UI with live waveform

symptoms: need to handle a flaky network during a long model download
want resumable downloads
root_cause: ONNX Runtime Web fetches files in one go; partial downloads abort and restart on retry. The HF CDN supports range requests, but Transformers.js doesn't currently use them.
fix: Catch network errors in the worker and post a 'network-error' state to UI
Show Retry button; users on flaky networks may need several attempts
For very large models, consider mirroring to a CDN you control with HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 for better recovery

symptoms: want to align Whisper output with original audio for playback
show karaoke-style highlight
root_cause: With return_timestamps: 'word', each chunk has a timestamp pair. Sync them with HTMLAudioElement currentTime to highlight the active word.
fix: Render words with data-start/data-end attributes
On playback timeupdate event, find the word whose [start, end] contains audio.currentTime
Update the highlighted word; throttle to ~10 Hz with requestAnimationFrame

symptoms: want to test the audio app locally with HTTPS
getUserMedia needs secure context
root_cause: localhost is a secure context, so getUserMedia works without HTTPS in dev. Custom hostnames (e.g. dev.local) need a self-signed cert.
fix: Stick to http://localhost:3000 for normal dev
For LAN testing, use mkcert to issue a local cert and run next dev --experimental-https
Never deploy a non-HTTPS preview that needs the mic — secure context is required

symptoms: Webpack 5 chunk-split warning when bundling worker
import inside worker fails at runtime
root_cause: When workers import shared modules, Webpack must emit a separate chunk graph for them. Misconfigured output.workerChunkLoading or output.workerPublicPath causes runtime resolution failures.
fix: In Next.js the defaults work — stick to new Worker(new URL('./worker.ts', import.meta.url), { type: 'module' })
Avoid dynamic-string Worker paths that hide the import from the bundler
If you customise webpack, leave output.workerChunkLoading and output.workerPublicPath at their defaults

symptoms: want to show useful errors when WebGPU init fails
users see generic 'Error' message
root_cause: WebGPU adapter request can fail for many reasons (driver, OS, blocklist). Surface a friendly fallback rather than a stack trace.
fix: Wrap pipeline init in try/catch; on failure log the error and re-init with { device: 'wasm' }
Tell the user 'GPU acceleration unavailable, using CPU' so they understand the slower experience
Don't auto-toggle device mid-session; reload the worker on user request to swap

symptoms: Vercel build complains about transformers.js eval
CSP violation from wasm code
root_cause: ONNX Runtime Web requires WebAssembly compilation, which violates strict CSP. Allow wasm-unsafe-eval in script-src.
fix: script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval' — required for ORT
Don't use 'unsafe-eval'; the wasm-specific directive is sufficient
Validate with the browser DevTools console; CSP violations are logged there

symptoms: want to show users the model is loaded and ready
no clear ready signal
root_cause: After pipeline() resolves, send a { type: 'ready' } message back to the UI so the record button becomes enabled.
fix: Worker: const pipe = await getPipe(); self.postMessage({ type: 'ready' })
UI: disable Record until ready=true
Persist ready state in component state and clear on worker reload

symptoms: want to keep recording while transcribing previous chunks
live transcription needs concurrent capture and inference
root_cause: Capture happens on the main thread (or AudioWorklet), inference in the worker. They run in parallel by design — just queue chunks into the worker without blocking capture.
fix: Maintain a chunk queue in the UI; postMessage each chunk as it's ready
Use a unique chunk id so out-of-order responses are still placed correctly
Bound the queue length; drop the oldest if the user records faster than the worker can transcribe

symptoms: want to detect when first chunk transcription completes
show 'first words appearing' state
root_cause: Use chunk ids and a Map<id, status> to track per-chunk progress and signal first completion.
fix: On send: pending.set(id, { startedAt: Date.now() })
On receive: const entry = pending.get(id); if (entry && !firstCompleted) { firstCompleted = true; onFirstChunk(entry) }
Use this hook to show a 'Processing audio…' -> 'Live transcription' transition

symptoms: want a fallback for browsers without WebAssembly
very old browser support
root_cause: Transformers.js requires WebAssembly. Browsers without it (very old IE-era) can't run inference at all.
fix: Detect: if (typeof WebAssembly === 'undefined') showUnsupportedUI()
Show a clear 'browser not supported' page for those users
Don't try to polyfill WebAssembly — it isn't a viable option

symptoms: want to expose a 'download all models' button
let users prefetch for offline
root_cause: Pipeline() warms the cache via the same fetch path. Calling it without performing inference is enough to populate IndexedDB.
fix: On 'Download for offline' click, call pipeline() with a progress_callback and discard the result
After resolution, mark localStorage flag and surface 'Ready offline' in the UI
Skip the prefetch on metered connections (navigator.connection?.saveData)

symptoms: want to show formatted transcript with paragraph breaks
raw text is one giant block
root_cause: Whisper returns punctuation but no paragraph breaks. Insert breaks at long pauses between chunks.
fix: Inspect chunk timestamps; if gap > 2s between chunks insert a paragraph break
Or detect end-of-utterance via ending punctuation (.!?)
Render with whitespace: pre-line so newlines flow visually

symptoms: want to avoid auto-scroll jank in long transcripts
user can't read because list keeps scrolling
root_cause: Naive auto-scroll on every token interrupts the user. Only auto-scroll when they're at the bottom.
fix: Track if scrollTop + clientHeight >= scrollHeight - threshold (e.g., 50 px)
Only call scrollIntoView when the user is at the bottom
Disable auto-scroll once user scrolls up; re-enable on next bottom-touch

symptoms: want to test audio capture without a mic
CI runs need a deterministic audio source
root_cause: Playwright/Chromium can be launched with --use-fake-ui-for-media-stream and --use-file-for-fake-audio-capture to feed a pre-recorded WAV.
fix: launchOptions.args = ['--use-fake-ui-for-media-stream', '--use-fake-device-for-media-stream', `--use-file-for-fake-audio-capture=${path}`]
Use a known good speech sample so transcripts are stable
Compare transcripts against a fuzzy-tolerant assertion (Levenshtein <= N edits)

symptoms: Next.js Turbopack: 'Error compiling worker'
new Worker(new URL(...), {type:'module'}) breaks on Turbopack
root_cause: Turbopack support for Web Workers has matured but some patterns still differ from Webpack. Verify on the Next.js version in use.
fix: Use the standard pattern: new Worker(new URL('./audio.worker.ts', import.meta.url), { type: 'module' })
If Turbopack fails, fall back to next dev with the default Webpack until you upgrade
Track the Next.js release notes for Turbopack worker support

symptoms: want to support installation as PWA
users want offline app icon on home screen
root_cause: An audio app benefits hugely from PWA install: full screen, no browser chrome, models cached for offline.
fix: Add manifest.json with name, icons, start_url, display: 'standalone'
Register a service worker that caches the JS shell; let Transformers.js manage IndexedDB
Show an Add-to-Home-Screen prompt after a successful first transcription

symptoms: want to react to model load progress in React state
progress bar bound to React
root_cause: The worker's progress messages are async; useState + a useEffect listener mirrors them into React state.
fix: const [progress, setProgress] = useState({ file: '', pct: 0 })

useEffect(() => { const h = (e) => e.data.type === 'progress' && setProgress({ file: e.data.file, pct: e.data.progress }); worker.addEventListener('message', h); return () => worker.removeEventListener('message', h) }, [])
Render <progress value={progress.pct} max={100} /> with the file name beside it

symptoms: want zero-network audio app on Vercel
minimise external dependencies
root_cause: Once weights are cached and the JS shell is offline, the app needs no network to function. Make this explicit in the build.
fix: Self-host ONNX Runtime Web wasm in /public/onnx
Self-host or mirror models to your origin or Vercel Blob
Add a service worker that serves the JS shell from cache-first

symptoms: want to release inference resources when user navigates away
memory leaks across page changes
root_cause: If the worker survives navigation but inference state grows, you can hit OOM after long sessions.
fix: Add a 'reset' message that disposes pipelines and resets caches in the worker
Send it on visibilitychange to hidden after a timeout (e.g., 5 min idle)
Recreate pipeline on next user action

symptoms: want to add accessibility for the transcription UI
screen reader compatibility
root_cause: Live regions inform screen readers when the transcript updates. Use aria-live='polite' and a separate visually-hidden region for completed text to avoid a flood.
fix: {transcript}
Update transcript in larger chunks (e.g., per finalised utterance) rather than per token to reduce SR noise
Provide a transcript download for users who prefer reading offline

symptoms: want to support keyboard shortcuts for record/stop
power users expect spacebar to toggle
root_cause: Bind a single key (e.g., spacebar when not in a text input) to start/stop recording for accessibility and power-user efficiency.
fix: Listen for keydown on document; check e.code === 'Space' && !inEditableContext
Prevent default to avoid scrolling the page
Show a hint in the UI: 'Press space to start/stop recording'

symptoms: Vercel preview deploys load model fine but production fails
model 404 only in production environment
root_cause: Different environment variables between preview and production can change env.remoteHost or env.localModelPath at runtime, breaking the URL.
fix: Audit Vercel env vars across Production/Preview/Development scopes
Make sure NEXT_PUBLIC_MODEL_HOST (if used) is set in all scopes
Pin a hardcoded remoteHost when behaviour differs unexpectedly to isolate the issue

symptoms: preflight before shipping audio app to Vercel
want a launch checklist
root_cause: Shipping a Transformers.js audio app on Vercel involves a recurring set of must-haves: SSR off, worker isolation, COOP/COEP, wasm path, model path, dtype + device strategy, mic UX, error states.
fix: AudioInterface dynamic-imported with { ssr: false }
next.config.mjs sets webpack fallback for fs/path/crypto on the client
Headers (vercel.json or next.config.mjs) emit COOP same-origin and COEP require-corp
env.backends.onnx.wasm.wasmPaths set to a stable URL (CDN or /onnx/)
Picked + tested dtype + device combo across mobile, mid-range laptop, and capable desktop
Microphone permission UX handles allow / deny / prompt
Progress UX shown for first cold load
No @huggingface/transformers import in any app/api/* route or server-rendered file
